title: strophostyles umbellata
intro: Strophostyles umbellata, also known as the hairy bush bean, is a perennial legume native to North America, characterized by its hairy stems and umbrella-shaped clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Strophostyles umbellata include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
